_meta.note: Daemon-backed immutable registry write. MCP accepts definition_json as a JSON-encoded RuleDefinition string; success returns only the assigned rule id and version. TB-3/TB-4: the tool and definition_json descriptions embed a complete kind:regex example, the REQUIRED fields {id, version, kind, severity, event_kind, summary_template, +pattern when kind=regex}, the kind and severity enums, the Draft default disclosure, and a pointer to registry_get for the canonical shape, so authoring a rule succeeds on the first try. event_kind has no serde default in RuleDefinition and must be listed in the REQUIRED set or the first call fails deserialization. Version is store-assigned: the submitted version is ignored, the store assigns version=latest+1, stamps it into the persisted definition, and returns it, so the returned version round-trips through activate/deactivate/list_active (no row-vs-definition version divergence).

invariants: definition_json is required and is a JSON string, not a nested object
RuleDefinition uses live field names including context_hint.before_lines and context_hint.after_lines
the definition_json description lists the REQUIRED fields {id, version, kind, severity, event_kind, summary_template, +pattern when kind=regex} so the first authoring call succeeds (TB-3)
event_kind is in the REQUIRED set because RuleDefinition.event_kind has no serde default and validate() rejects an empty event_kind; omitting it fails the first call

the description surfaces the kind enum (keyword|regex|prompt|exit_code|stream_marker|progress_collapse|dedupe|threshold|sequence|anchor|custom) and severity enum (trace|debug|info|low|medium|high|critical)
the description discloses that new rules default to status=Draft and that status:active is needed for registry_activate eligibility (TB-4)
the description points to registry_get as the canonical RuleDefinition shape
success payload does not echo the rule definition
invalid definitions fail instead of being silently accepted

the submitted version is ignored; the store assigns version=latest+1, stamps it into the persisted definition, and returns it, so the returned version is the one activate/deactivate/list_active accept (no row-vs-definition version divergence)
